scenarios_detailed.reasoning: AIX 7.2 is OUTDATED with standard support ended. Security patches are limited to extended support contracts, increasing vulnerability exposure for a High-criticality Confidential application.
scenarios_detailed.modernization_suggestion: Update AIX to the latest supported version or plan migration to a standard Linux distribution to reduce proprietary platform risk.

scenarios_detailed.scenario_name: Switch to Standard Linux Operating System
scenarios_detailed.reasoning: AIX 7.2 is a proprietary IBM UNIX platform with limited vendor ecosystem support. Migrating to RHEL 9 or similar standard Linux would reduce licensing costs and broaden tooling availability. However, COBOL portability must be validated.
scenarios_detailed.modernization_suggestion: Plan a phased migration from AIX 7.2 to RHEL 9. Validate COBOL compiler compatibility (GnuCOBOL or IBM COBOL for Linux) before migrating.

scenarios_detailed.reasoning: COBOL-2014 on AIX 7.2 is tightly coupled to IBM Power architecture. COBOL compilers with full ARM64 support are limited, and the application has no CI/CD or containerization to facilitate cross-architecture builds.
scenarios_detailed.modernization_suggestion: This scenario is blocked until the application is modernized to a portable language/runtime stack and containerized.

scenarios_detailed.reasoning: ERPApp-001 does not use an application server (application_server: None). The COBOL application runs directly on AIX without a middleware tier.
scenarios_detailed.modernization_suggestion: Not applicable. No application server is present to replace.

scenarios_detailed.scenario_name: Application Migration to Cloud Infrastructure (Lift & Shift)
scenarios_detailed.reasoning: ERPApp-001 is currently On-Premise with no cloud footprint. A lift-and-shift migration to cloud (e.g., IBM Power Virtual Server on IBM Cloud, or AWS Bare Metal) would improve scalability and reduce on-premise hardware costs.
scenarios_detailed.modernization_suggestion: Evaluate IBM Power Virtual Server (IBM Cloud) as an AIX-compatible lift-and-shift target to maintain COBOL/AIX compatibility while moving to cloud.

scenarios_detailed.reasoning: COBOL on AIX 7.2 with a 1-Tier monolithic architecture and no CI/CD pipeline is not suitable for containerization without major refactoring. There is no container runtime or orchestration infrastructure in place.
scenarios_detailed.modernization_suggestion: Containerization is blocked until the COBOL application is refactored or rewritten to a portable technology stack and a CI/CD pipeline is established.

scenarios_detailed.reasoning: The 1-Tier monolithic COBOL architecture with 5 external interfaces and a 2027 decommission target strongly suggests refactoring is necessary. Breaking the monolith into services would enable incremental modernization.
scenarios_detailed.modernization_suggestion: Conduct a COBOL code analysis (using tools like Micro Focus, IBM Rational) to identify service boundaries. Gradually expose business functions as APIs to enable phased modernization.

scenarios_detailed.reasoning: Oracle 19c is the current LTS version with support through April 2027. No database upgrade is needed at this time.
scenarios_detailed.modernization_suggestion: Monitor Oracle 19c end-of-extended-support (April 2027) and plan upgrade to Oracle 21c or 23ai before that date.

scenarios_detailed.scenario_name: Switch DB Engine to Open-Source Database Solution
scenarios_detailed.reasoning: Oracle 19c requires a commercial license (database_license_required: true). Migrating to PostgreSQL or other open-source alternatives would eliminate Oracle licensing costs. However, Oracle-specific SQL and PL/SQL must be migrated.
scenarios_detailed.modernization_suggestion: Evaluate AWS Aurora PostgreSQL or standard PostgreSQL as migration targets. Use tools like AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT) or Ora2Pg to assess migration complexity.

scenarios_detailed.reasoning: AIX 7.2 is OUTDATED. Updating to the latest AIX 7.3 or planning OS migration addresses security gaps and extends vendor support coverage for this High-criticality application.
scenarios_detailed.modernization_suggestion: Engage IBM support to update AIX 7.2 to AIX 7.3 if hardware permits, or plan OS migration. Review COBOL compiler and runtime versions for available updates.
